sections.paragraph:
-

(1) pr. i.q. שָׁמַץ to smite, to strike; also, to cast, to throw down, compare Arab. شمص to strike, to thrust, and to urge on a beast violently. (To this answers the Germ. vulg. fchmeißen, to strike and to cast; Anglo-Sax. smitan; Engl. to smite; rejecting the sibilant, mittere.) Hence-(a) 2Sa 6:6, כִּי שָֽׁמְטוּ הַבָּקָר “for the oxen kicked,” were restive (die Rinder fchlugen, fchmißen aus). Vulg. calcitrabant. The other interpretations of this passage are discussed by Bochart, Hieroz. t. i. page 372.-(b) to cast, to throw down (any one from a window into the street), 2Ki 9:33.

(2) to fall, to let lie-(a) a field untilled, Exo 23:11.-(b) to remit a debt, Deu 15:2.-(c) followed by מִן to desist from anything, Jer 17:4.

Niphal, pass. of Kal No. 1, to be cast down, precipitated (from a rock), Psa 141:6.

Hiphil, i.q. Kal No. 2, b, to remit, Deu 15:3. Hence-
